channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Pré-candidato ao Planalto, Zema renuncia em MG, e Simões assume o governo
item.description: Romeu Zema resigned as governor of Minas Gerais to focus on his presidential pre-candidacy. His vice, Mateus Simões de Almeida, assumed office and pledged balance between powers, criticizing the Lula government and the Mais Médicos program. Simões also announced infrastructure works on a federal bridge, blaming São Paulo’s government for the costs.

item.title: Eleições municipais na França: participação nas primeiras horas de votação tem ligeiro neste 2° turno
item.description: Approximately 20.33% of French voters had cast their ballots by noon on Sunday (22) in the second round of municipal elections across over 1,500 municipalities. Turnout was described as slightly below initial expectations, indicating moderate voter engagement. The figures were released by France's Ministry of the Interior.

item.title: As estrelas da política que já estão confirmadas em filiação de Moro ao PL
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro will join the PL on Tuesday in a move led by Valdemar Costa Neto, with expected participation from Flávio Bolsonaro, who aims to use Paraná as a base for his presidential campaign. Moro’s shift follows his departure from União Brasil due to lack of space to run for Paraná governor, and the likely Senate candidates on his ticket are Deltan Dallagnol (Novo) and Filipe Barros (PL).

item.title: Os 7% que podem inclinar a balança (por Gaudêncio Torquato)
item.description: With 196 days until the 2026 presidential election, approximately 7% of Brazil's electorate identifies as independent, forming a decisive bloc in a context of intense polarization and technical ties between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. This voter, not necessarily ideologically moderate, is pragmatic and responsive to the economy, health, security, and ethics, basing their vote on governmental performance rather than party loyalty.

item.title: Quem é Mateus Simões, novo governador de Minas Gerais e herdeiro político de Zema
item.description: Mateus Simões, the vice-governor of Minas Gerais, assumes the governorship following the resignation of Romeu Zema, who is running for President of Brazil in 2026. The inauguration takes place this Sunday (22) at the Minas Gerais Legislative Assembly, followed by the official transfer of power at the Palácio da Liberdade. A member of the PSD, Simões is portrayed as Zema’s political heir, with a career in state public administration and a key role as an articulator of the current administration.

item.title: Mesmo sem receber apoio, Flávio afaga Michelle no dia do aniversário dela
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) congratulated Michelle Bolsonaro on social media for her birthday, despite her lack of engagement in his presidential pre-campaign. The gesture was seen as a personal affirmation, detached from the political context of mutual support. Michelle’s limited involvement in Flávio’s campaign has been noted in right-wing political circles.

item.title: Lula tem que ‘mostrar o que tem feito’ para vencer Flávio em 2026, diz deputado do PT
item.description: State deputy Emídio de Souza (PT-SP) said President Lula must showcase his accomplishments from his third term to defeat Flávio Bolsonaro in the 2026 elections. He emphasized that the government needs to effectively communicate its achievements, such as the income tax exemption for those earning up to R$ 5,000 monthly, while the opposition has not presented an alternative program. Souza also noted that Brazil’s presidential race has become highly polarized and tightly contested, resembling the U.S. two-party dynamic, where small details can decisively influence the outcome.

item.title: Itália vai às urnas neste domingo em 1º dia de referendo
item.description: Italy opened polling stations for the first day of a referendum on a judicial reform proposed by Giorgia Meloni's government. Voting continues on Monday, with over 51 million eligible voters, including citizens abroad. Turnout so far stands at 14%, with the highest participation in northern regions, particularly Emilia-Romagna.

item.title: Indicação de Messias ao STF só tem 25 apoios declarados no Senado
item.description: The nomination of Jorge Messias to the Supreme Federal Court has only 25 declared supporters in the Senate, falling short of the 41 votes needed. Before a full Senate vote, he must be approved by the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), where he has 10 of the required 14 votes, with 7 opposed and 10 undeclared. Lula is working to build political backing, including potentially nominating Rodrigo Pacheco for Minas Gerais governor, despite Pacheco having been previously backed by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre for the STF seat.

item.title: Saiba quais senadores dizem apoiar Messias no STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has nominated Jorge Messias to the STF, with 25 senators declaring support, leaving 16 more votes needed to reach the 41 required. Before the full Senate vote, Messias must be approved by the CCJ, where he already has 10 of the 14 required votes. Of the 81 senators surveyed by Poder360, 14 oppose, 39 have not declared a position, and 3 did not respond.

item.title: Saiba quais senadores dizem ser contra Messias no STF
item.description: Lula's nominee to the STF, Jorge Messias, has declared support from 25 senators but needs 16 more votes to reach the 41 required for approval. In the CCJ, he has 10 of the 14 needed votes, with 7 senators opposed and 10 undecided. Of the 81 senators surveyed by Poder360, 14 declared opposition to Messias, 39 did not state a position, and 3 did not respond.

item.title: Itália vota em referendo uma controversa reforma judiciária
item.description: Italians are voting in a referendum on a judicial reform proposed by Giorgia Meloni's government, which seeks to separate the careers of judges and prosecutors and reform the Superior Council of the Magistracy. The government claims the reform will make the judiciary more meritocratic and effective, while opponents denounce it as an attack on judicial independence and a political maneuver. The vote, taking place on March 22-23, may become a referendum on Meloni's leadership ahead of the 2027 legislative elections.

item.title: Kim Kataguiri diz que Moraes tem que apodrecer na cadeia
item.description: Federal deputy Kim Kataguiri delivered a confrontational speech at an MBL-organized anti-corruption march on Paulista Avenue, demanding that Supreme Court ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli 'rot in prison'. He linked the ministers to alleged misconduct involving banker Daniel Vorcaro and claimed any legal action against him stems from comments about Senator Weverton Rocha, not corruption charges. Kataguiri said he would accept imprisonment as a matter of principle and accused the Lula government and STF of weaponizing state institutions against opponents.

item.title: Pesquisas eleitorais: o que dizem os últimos números da disputa entre Lula e Flávio
item.description: Recent polls show a technical tie between Lula (47.4%) and Flávio Bolsonaro (45.3%) in the 2026 presidential race, within a 2.5-point margin of error. Bolsonaro (PL) is gaining momentum while Lula struggles with negative government evaluations on inflation, security, and spending. Both candidates face high rejection rates (~55-56%), and no third-way candidate exceeds 10% support in a highly polarized environment.

item.title: Clarita Maia na Crusoé: A Corte Interamericana enfrenta o perigo das reeleições
item.description: Clarita Maia, in Crusoé, warns of the risks of presidential reelections in Latin America, noting that while initially adopted to extend popular mandates, they are now seen as threats to power alternation and enablers of authoritarian concentration, as seen in Venezuela. The Inter-American Court of Human Rights faces the challenge of assessing whether such practices violate regional democratic norms.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro inicia pré-campanha no Nordeste
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) launched his presidential pre-campaign in the Northeast, starting in Natal, where the PL party formalized its majoritarian ticket for Rio Grande do Norte. Former mayor Álvaro Dias was confirmed as the gubernatorial candidate, with Babá Pereira as his running mate. The Senate slate was also announced, though specific names were not detailed in the provided excerpt.

item.title: Mendonça e Vorcaro: as "estrelas" que a eleição não esperava para 2026
item.description: Minister André Mendonça, rapporteur of the Master Case, and banker Daniel Vorcaro, whose plea bargain exposes an alleged billion-dollar fraud linked to the Three Powers, have become central figures in the 2026 elections despite not being candidates. Mendonça seeks discretion but controls the political timeline of the case, while Vorcaro’s revelations could undermine candidacies and rewrite the presidential succession script.

item.title: O TSE de 2026: quem são os ministros e o que esperar da corte na eleição
item.description: The TSE in 2026 will have new leadership starting in June, with Kassio Nunes Marques as president and André Mendonça as vice-president, marking the first time two ministers appointed by Bolsonaro to the STF lead the court simultaneously. The tribunal will face challenges including the implementation of the Clean Record Law and monitoring AI-generated content, while maintaining its role in organizing and supervising elections, reviewing campaign finances, and adjudicating electoral disputes.

item.title: Lula chega a Campo Grande para COP15 e recebe líderes internacionais
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva arrived in Campo Grande to attend the 15th Conference of the Parties to the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species (COP15), taking place from March 23 to 29. The summit brings together international leaders, including Paraguayan President Santiago Peña and Brazilian ministers such as Marina Silva and Sonia Guajajara. Brazil is presiding over the conference, aiming to strengthen its global environmental leadership.

item.title: Frase do dia
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized foreign interventions in other countries, citing Cuba, Venezuela, Iran, and Iraq as examples, and questioned the legitimacy of justifications such as weapons of mass destruction. He argued that no nation has the right to claim ownership over others and defended national sovereignty as a core principle of international democracy.

item.title: Jornalista que investigou Dino recebe convites de dois partidos
item.description: Journalist Luís Pablo Conceição Almeida received invitations to join União Brasil and the PRD to run for deputy in the 2026 elections, after being targeted by the Federal Police for publishing reports on the irregular use of official vehicles from TJ-MA by relatives of Flávio Dino. Despite the offers, he declined and stated he intends to continue working as a journalist, a profession he inherited from his father in Maranhão.

item.title: Eleitor não atribui ganhos ao governo, diz Manente, do Cidadania
item.description: Alex Manente, president of the Cidadania party, said the Lula government fails to gain voter approval because citizens see it as merely one part of a larger system, not crediting it for policies like the IR exemption up to R$5,000. He noted internal party conflicts with factions tied to the party's PCB past, but affirmed that the March 2026 congress solidified his leadership. The party aims to act as a dignified, ethical opposition in the 2026 elections, fielding competitive slates nationwide.

item.title: Trump tenta vender Copa a conservadores avessos ao futebol
item.description: Donald Trump is attempting to persuade his conservative base to embrace the 2026 World Cup, despite the group's historical aversion to soccer, which was once criticized as 'socialist' or a sign of national moral decline. The United States, Canada, and Mexico will host the tournament this year, reflecting a shifting conservative perception of the sport in the U.S.

item.title: Portal de Dados Abertos do TSE amplia acesso a informações eleitorais
item.description: The TSE Open Data Portal expands access to electoral information, providing hundreds of datasets on candidacies, electorate, results, and party and candidate finances. The initiative supports academic research, journalism, and the fight against disinformation by promoting transparency and enabling verification of electoral claims.

item.title: Secretária de Boulos vira aposta da "velha guarda" do PT para Alesp
item.description: Jessy Dayane, deputy secretary of Youth in the General Secretariat of the Presidency of the Republic, is being promoted as a key bet by the 'old guard' of the Workers' Party (PT) to run for a seat in the São Paulo Legislative Assembly in 2026. She is backed by historical PT figures such as Zé Dirceu, Rui Falcão, Edinho Silva, and José Genoino, and previously served in Lula’s transition team. Dayane has a background in student movements, was UNE vice-president from 2017 to 2019, holds a law degree and a master’s in Public Security from FGV.

item.title: Lula vai à COP15 para ampliar adesão à convenção ambiental
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva opened the high-level session of COP15 in Campo Grande, marking the first time Brazil hosts and presides over the conference on migratory species. The event, running through March 29, aims to expand convention membership through the Pantanal Declaration and includes initiatives to protect species such as the toninha and bagre, while strengthening cooperation with Bolivia and Paraguay.

item.title: Athletico-PR e Coritiba fazem o clássico na elite do Brasileirão após três anos
item.description: Athletico-PR and Coritiba will face off this Sunday at Arena da Baixada in Curitiba in the traditional Athletiba derby, valid for the eighth round of the Brasileirão. This marks the first meeting between the two teams in the top flight after three years, as both returned to Série A in 2025 following relegations in 2023 and 2024. Historically, Coritiba leads with 136 wins to Athletico’s 114 and 104 draws in 355 matches, with the Série A record tied at 16 wins each and 12 draws in 44 games.

item.title: Michelle tem duas semanas para decidir se viajará o Brasil por Flávio
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro has two weeks to decide whether to travel across Brazil in support of Flávio Bolsonaro’s presidential pre-campaign. While her Senate candidacy is seen as certain, she has avoided active engagement in her son’s campaign, drawing criticism and mounting pressure from allies. The closing of the party window on April 4 and the end of Jair Bolsonaro’s protective directive heighten the urgency for a clear stance.

item.title: Lula convoca aliados históricos para campanha e cobra gabinete de ‘pronta- resposta’ a Flávio
item.description: Lula summoned historic allies to coordinate his campaign for a fourth term, demanding a 'rapid-response' team to counter Flávio Bolsonaro, his main adversary. The PT’s strategy includes labeling the senator as 'a coup supporter like his father', fearing his rise could lead to Tarcísio de Freitas being nominated as the bolsonarist candidate, seen as more dangerous. The campaign aims to overcome the government’s low approval (32-33%) despite positive economic indicators.

item.title: Lula vive fadiga política após disputar tantas eleições, admite entorno do presidente
item.description: Lula faces political fatigue after running in presidential elections since 1989, with image wear reflected in popularity polls. His government seeks to boost approval through inaugurations of projects, such as school buses in Minas Gerais and initiatives in Rio de Janeiro, within the electoral window allowing inaugurations until July 4. The October election is seen as a plebiscite on another Lula term, with his positive approval at 33%, below the threshold needed for re-election or succession.

item.title: Franceses votam no segundo turno de eleições municipais; disputa é acirrada em Paris e Marselha
item.description: French voters began casting ballots in the second round of municipal elections on Sunday (22), with tight races in major cities including Paris, Marseille, Lyon, and Toulouse. The election unfolds amid a fragmented left and rising far-right influence, leaving outcomes uncertain across several municipalities.

item.title: Sem encontro entre Alcolumbre e Lula, sabatina de Messias pode ficar para depois das eleições
item.description: The potential absence of a face-to-face meeting between President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Senate President Davi Alcolumbre could delay the vote on Jorge Messias’ nomination to the STF until after the October elections. Tensions between the two, fueled by Alcolumbre’s dissatisfaction with Messias’ selection and ongoing investigations into Banco Master, have led to a breakdown in communication and reduced Senate activity. Despite occasional contacts, no meeting has been scheduled to resolve the nomination, which remains stalled due to lack of consensus between the Planalto Palace and Senate leadership.

item.title: Com status de presidenciáveis, Tarcísio e Haddad reeditam duelo em SP
item.description: The 2026 São Paulo gubernatorial election will reprise the duel between Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) and Fernando Haddad (PT), who faced off in the 2022 runoff. Tarcísio, the current governor with high approval ratings and positioned as a right-wing presidential contender, challenges Haddad, the Finance Minister who delivered solid economic results amid a challenging political landscape. The race is defined by contrasting ideological positions, with Tarcísio linked to moderate bolsonarismo and Haddad representing the continuation of the PT's project.

item.title: Opção ao Senado por SP, deputado patrocina post contra Erika Hilton
item.description: State deputy Gil Diniz (PL-SP) is sponsoring social media posts that criticize the gender identity and policy positions of federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSol-SP), including unfounded claims about gender transition in minors and abortion legalization for minors. These posts may violate TSE Resolution No. 23.610/2019, which bans political propaganda containing prejudice based on sexual orientation or gender identity. Diniz is backed by allies of Eduardo Bolsonaro to run for Senate on Tarcísio de Freitas's ticket.

item.title: As pautas paradas no Senado por conta de rusga de Lula e Alcolumbre
item.description: The rift between President Lula and Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, triggered by Lula's nomination of Jorge Messias to the STF, has stalled key government agendas in Congress, including the Public Security PEC and a bill offering tax incentives for data centers. Alcolumbre has not yet sent the Security PEC to the Constitution and Justice Committee nor scheduled the data center bill for vote, despite its approval in the Chamber. The government is revising its strategy to advance these proposals amid the political deadlock.

item.title: Potência municipal e nos estados, PSD se consolida no plano nacional com ‘equilíbrio pragmático’
item.description: After 15 years since its founding, the PSD has become the country's leading municipal force with 891 mayors elected in 2024 and holds the highest number of governors, including three presidential pre-candidates: Ratinho Junior (PR), Eduardo Leite (RS) and Ronaldo Caiado (GO). The party aims to position itself as a third way in the polarization between Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), with Gilberto Kassab leading the articulation and the presidential nominee to be announced by March 25.

item.title: Abelardo de la Espriella rechaza el apoyo de los partidos tradicionales para preservar su imagen de ‘outsider’
item.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, a far-right candidate, formally rejected support from traditional parties to preserve his 'outsider' image. In a statement, he said that over four months, leaders of the 'old politics' approached him to join his campaign, but were consistently refused. The move comes as traditional blocs without their own presidential candidate debate whom to back in the May 31 elections.

item.title: Abel dá aula a Roger, Palmeiras bate o São Paulo freguesia e dispara na ponta do Brasileiro
item.description: Palmeiras defeated São Paulo 1-0, with Jhon Arias scoring in the 5th minute, extending their recent dominance in the rivalry and opening a six-point lead at the top of the 2026 Brasileirão. Abel Ferreira showcased tactical superiority, neutralizing São Paulo's attack despite 63% possession, while Roger Machado faces growing criticism after losses to Atlético-MG and Palmeiras, with São Paulo's leadership questioning his ability to deliver a title this season.

item.title: Famílias comprometem 29% da renda com dívidas, maior patamar em 20 anos
item.description: Brazilian families are allocating 29% of their income to debt, the highest level in 20 years, despite unemployment at historic lows and inflation under control. This situation raises alarms in financial institutions, retailers, and even in the 2026 electoral race, making household indebtedness a central political issue.

item.title: La CELAC mira a África mientras se debilita por dentro
item.description: CELAC held its summit in Bogotá, featuring the first high-level meeting with Africa and the handover of the pro tempore presidency from Colombia to Uruguay. Uruguayan President Yamandú Orsi reaffirmed the bloc’s consensus on maintaining Latin America as a zone of peace, while Colombian President Gustavo Petro transferred the symbolic authority. Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Burundian President Évariste Ndayishimiye, current AU Chair, also attended.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro inicia pré-campanha no NE com defesa de domiciliar para Jair e discurso anti-PT
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a presidential pre-candidate, launched his pre-campaign in the Northeast defending house arrest for his father, Jair Bolsonaro, and intensifying criticism of the Lula (PT) government. His speech emphasized an anti-PT stance and focused on security and justice issues, aiming to consolidate support in the region.

item.title: Em 1º discurso no Nordeste, Flávio faz novo aceno às mulheres e critica segurança pública do PT
item.description: In his first speech in the Northeast as a presidential pre-candidate, Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) appealed to female voters and criticized Lula’s government on public safety, citing rising femicide rates. He advocated for zero tolerance against aggressors of women and framed the election as a choice between prosperity and criminality, claiming the PT has governed Rio Grande do Norte for too long without improving citizens’ lives. Flávio emphasized women as a campaign priority, despite polls showing most women fear his potential election.

item.title: Lula e Petro discutem relações regionais em reunião na Celac
item.description: President Lula met with Gustavo Petro at the Celac summit to discuss Petro's presidency of the meeting and Uruguay's leadership in the organization, while reaffirming the importance of regional integration as a historical necessity and South America as a zone of peace. He also met with Burundi's president to discuss Michelle Bachelet's candidacy for UN Secretary-General, congratulated Burundi on its African Union presidency, and addressed cooperation on critical minerals and rare earths to prevent neo-extractivism.

item.title: Em ataque a Lula, Flávio mira no crime e tenta se afastar do machismo
item.description: In Flávio Bolsonaro's first pre-campaign event in the Northeast, held in Natal, he focused his speech on attacking President Lula's government, emphasizing public security and crime, while avoiding concrete proposals for regional development. His strategy aimed to distance himself from accusations of machismo, attempting to reposition his image before Northeastern voters.

item.title: MBL realiza marcha contra corrupção na Paulista
item.description: The Brazil Free Movement (MBL) held an anti-corruption march on Paulista Avenue in São Paulo, proceeding to Sé Square. The event featured pre-candidates and politicians including Renan Santos, Kim Kataguiri, Guto Zacarias, and Amanda Vettorazzo, who spoke in favor of stricter oversight of public funds and referenced STF ministers with the slogan 'the law also applies to those who wear the robe'. The MBL announced future mobilizations, with the next act scheduled for April 4.

item.title: Lula se encontra com líderes da Colômbia e Burundi durante Fórum Celac-África
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva met with Colombian President Gustavo Petro on the sidelines of the Celac-Africa High-Level Forum in Bogotá. They assessed Colombia’s leadership of Celac and discussed expectations for Uruguay’s upcoming presidency in the bloc, reaffirming the importance of strengthening regional multilateral institutions.

item.title: Atuação de titular do Botafogo chama atenção após vitória fora de casa no Brasileirão
item.description: Botafogo defeated Red Bull Bragantino 2-1 in the 7th round of the Brasileirão, temporarily escaping the relegation zone, with goals from Alex Telles and Alexander Barboza. Goalkeeper Raúl delivered a standout performance with two crucial saves, earning praise from fans and the club’s official profile. Botafogo’s next match is against Athletico-PR on Sunday, March 29, at 6:30 PM, in a rescheduled 5th-round fixture at Arena da Baixada.

item.title: Apoiadores cantam parabéns para Jair Bolsonaro em frente a hospital. Veja vídeo
item.description: Supporters of Jair Bolsonaro celebrated his 71st birthday outside DF Star Hospital in Brasília, where he remains in the ICU due to bacterial bronchopneumonia. Around 50 people sang 'Happy Birthday,' brought food, prayed, and chanted 'amnesty now,' while his sons and former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro visited him. Bolsonaro was sentenced by the STF to 27 years and 4 months in prison for coup attempt and still has no discharge date.

item.title: Ligações de Vorcaro expõem riscos de autoridades dos Três Poderes
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Banco Master, signed a confidentiality agreement with the Federal Police and the PGR, initiating a potential plea bargain for financial system fraud. His connections with authorities across the Three Powers — including meetings with Guido Mantega and Marcola at the Planalto, encounters with Alexandre de Moraes of the STF, and alleged information leaks by former Central Bank officials — expose risks of institutional compromise. The plea deal could implicate politicians, ministers, and high-ranking officials, with potential impact on the 2026 election.

item.title: Morre Robert Mueller, ex-FBI que apurou ligação da campanha de Trump com a Rússia
item.description: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel who investigated alleged collusion between Donald Trump's campaign and Russia, died at age 81 on Friday night, according to a family statement. Trump reacted on his Truth Social platform by celebrating Mueller's death, saying he was glad Mueller was dead so he could no longer 'hurt innocent people'. Mueller led the investigation from 2017 to 2019, which did not find sufficient evidence to charge Trump with conspiracy, but his report did not fully exonerate him.
